reference_gate.caveat: 6 residual reference failures share one click.testing environment-compatibility pattern and 4 skips are Windows-only cases; accepted by judge under the >=95% solvability threshold. These nodeids must not be counted as model-failure evidence.

notes: 2026-07-09 shipping sync: previous tasks/ contents (reconstructed remote score 66/264 whose own MANIFEST admitted 'not rerun', a 468-byte reference stub, a stale diagnosis report, and a mojibake-damaged spec copy) moved to archive/superseded-20260709/cookiecutter-fullrepro-001/. This package now carries the 2026-07-04 judge-certified WSL repair chain (reference 277/287, candidate 72/273). The 2026-07-03 'reconstructed, not rerun' user-authorized exception is thereby retired.

spec.md is byte-identical to candidate-runs/codex-cookiecutter-specv2only-20260629-001/output/spec_v2.md (what the candidate saw). Known historical wart retained verbatim: the packet header includes 'Status: candidate-visible public packet' and 'Task id: cookiecutter-fullrepro-001' lines that predate the cleanroom forbidden-word gate. Altering the shipped spec would desynchronize it from the scored run; flagged as open item for the next legitimate respec/re-evaluation cycle.

Denominator note: kept_nodeids.txt lists 222 base nodeids; parametrization expands to 287 cases on the reference environment and 273 on the candidate run (collection errors collapse some parametrizations). Numbers 264/222/273/287 seen in older documents refer to superseded runs; the shipped pair is 287 (reference) / 273 (candidate).
judge/ subdirectory retains 2026-06-29 historical gate reports predating the repair; they are context, not the certified verdict.
